methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Airship Industries is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The relevant peer universe comprises 7 private companies operating in this space, including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable here. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only 5 same-sector peers total, and none satisfy the filtering criteria for stage-alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, or valuation-tier similarity required to establish a reliable comparable set. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models and development stages across the broader universe.

Without a sufficiently dense peer cohort, this analysis cannot support a defensible valuation range through triangulation methods. A complete valuation would require either: (1) expansion of comparable set criteria to include loosely adjacent autonomous systems companies; (2) application of venture-stage cost-of-capital or dilution-based approaches; or (3) revenue multiple analysis if financial metrics are available. The core constraint is data scarcity rather than analytical method—the Drones & Autonomous category remains underpopulated relative to conventional VC sector density thresholds.
